meaning: n.拖着脚走,混乱,蒙混,洗纸牌v.拖曳,搅乱,慢吞吞地走,推诿,洗牌
examples: You have to shuffle the cards before the cut for the deal.
If you shuffle that deck enough times, the orders will have to repeat.
Purists say that a further drop in house prices would merely shuffle wealth around.
The Ali shuffle, once a prelude to devastating one-two punches, now mean something entirely different.
Shortly after noon recently at the University of Miami, students shuffle into a classroom.
